repository.name: Jatai
repository.description: The local micro-email and messaging bus for your file system. Connect scripts and AI agents instantly using a zero-config drop-folder pattern. Jataí uses OS file events to route data across directories via standardized INBOX/OUTBOX folders, without complex APIs or sockets. Drop a file, and it's delivered!

commit.message:
Release v0.2.16

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix the accuracy issue by update celu formula based on spec (#33500)

### Details:
- The implementation didn't align with [ONNX
spec](https://onnx.ai/onnx/operators/onnx__Celu.html).
```
max(0,x) + min(0,alpha*(exp(x/alpha)-1))
```
 

### Tickets:
 - [CVS-178958](https://jira.devtools.intel.com/browse/CVS-178958)

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
TECHDEBT-35: Fix VultronEvent to carry rich domain objects

Replace the flat ID/type string fields on VultronEvent with 7 rich
VultronObject | None fields (object_, target, context, origin,
inner_object, inner_target, inner_context) that mirror the AS2 Activity
structure at the core layer.

Changes:
- events/base.py: Replace 14 flat fields with 7 VultronObject fields;
  add derived @property for all old names (backward compat); object_
  uses trailing underscore because 'object' is a Python built-in
- events/_mixins.py: Add rich 'foo' property alongside each 'foo_id'
  property; properties return self.object_, self.target, etc. with
  domain-typed hints under TYPE_CHECKING
- event subclasses (report, case, embargo, note, status, case_participant,
  actor): Remove explicit typed domain fields (report: VultronReport,
  case: VultronCase, etc.) — now provided by mixin properties
- extractor.py: Update _build_domain_kwargs() to use object_= instead of
  per-semantic keys; add _to_domain_obj() helper; remove all flat _id/_type
  kwargs from constructor call; import VultronObject
- test_report_use_cases.py: Update 3 event constructors to use object_=

All 988 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(release): harden media runtime and publish packaging

Why: ingest outputs could overwrite each other, audio/video capability checks drifted between runtime and providers, and the publish pipeline could fail lint or ship stale dist artifacts.

Why: this keeps the validated 0.2.1 release changes together and makes the npm publish path reproducible.

commit.message:
refactor: TECHDEBT-39 — remove _find_and_update_participant_rm wrapper (OPP-05)

Removed the module-level _find_and_update_participant_rm() wrapper from
vultron/core/behaviors/report/nodes.py. Both TransitionParticipantRMtoAccepted
and TransitionParticipantRMtoDeferred now call update_participant_rm_state()
directly, inlining the bool→Status conversion and exception handling.

Removed redundant local 'from vultron.core.states.rm import RM' imports
inside each update() method — RM is already imported at module level.

There is now exactly one implementation of the 'append a new participant RM
status' operation: update_participant_rm_state() in triggers/_helpers.py,
used by both BT nodes and trigger use cases (SvcEngageCaseUseCase,
SvcDeferCaseUseCase).

988 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Release v0.2.14

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: TECHDEBT-34 — add EM state transition guards to embargo use cases

Three unguarded direct em_state = EM.ACTIVE assignments in vultron/core/
now have explicit machine guards:

- SvcEvaluateEmbargoUseCase (trigger-side): replaced direct assignment with
  EMAdapter + create_em_machine() + adapter.accept() pattern. MachineError
  raises VultronConflictError. Consistent with SvcProposeEmbargoUseCase
  and SvcTerminateEmbargoUseCase.

- AddEmbargoEventToCaseReceivedUseCase (receive-side): added
  is_valid_em_transition() check with WARNING log on non-standard
  transition; proceeds regardless (documented state-sync override).

- AcceptInviteToEmbargoOnCaseReceivedUseCase (receive-side): same pattern.

All rm_state=RM.XXX in core are constructor args for new status objects
(initial-state constructions, not transitions). The append_rm_state()
guard already enforces is_valid_rm_transition() for all RM mutation paths.

Added 4 tests (2 updated + 2 new warning tests + 1 conflict error test).
988 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Release v0.2.13

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: update README and SETUP_GUIDE for LLM tool-calling and configure_azure_monitor

- README: document bind_tools architecture, trace semantics table,
  custom metadata headers, KQL query, example curl
- SETUP_GUIDE: document configure_azure_monitor telemetry stack,
  observed trace shape with gen_ai.tool.definitions, az CLI query

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: use LLM tool-calling (bind_tools) for gen_ai.tool.definitions

Replace direct execute_tool() calls with llm.bind_tools() + tool execution
loop. The LLM now receives tool schemas and decides which tools to call.
This produces gen_ai.tool.definitions on chat spans in App Insights.

- tools.py: use @tool decorator, accept JSON string for plan arg
- graph.py: _invoke_with_tools() sends tool definitions to the LLM,
  executes tool_calls from the response, feeds ToolMessages back
- tests: StubLLM supports bind_tools() and returns tool_calls

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove traceparent from metadata to enable HTTP span nesting

When traceparent/tracestate were in metadata, the AzureAIOpenTelemetryTracer
extracted them via _extract_trace_headers() and called use_propagated_context(),
creating a NEW root context that bypassed the FastAPI HTTP span. Now the
callback inherits the OTel context from the FastAPI ASGI instrumentor,
so the HTTP request span becomes the tree root.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use configure_azure_monitor() for reliable App Insights export

Replace manual TracerProvider + BatchSpanProcessor with the Azure Monitor
OpenTelemetry distro (configure_azure_monitor). This handles:
- FastAPI HTTP auto-instrumentation
- Proper span export to App Insights
- LiveMetrics/QuickPulse
- Correct sampler configuration

Override OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER=always_on for demos to avoid 10% sampling.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

plan: mark TECHDEBT-32c complete in IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md and IMPLEMENTATION_HISTORY.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add FastAPI HTTP instrumentation for e2e trace visibility

Adds opentelemetry-instrumentation-fastapi so inbound HTTP requests
produce server spans in App Insights. The HTTP span becomes the root
of the trace tree, with all GenAI agent/tool spans nested under it.

commit.message:
Release v0.2.12

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(flutter): bind custom viewModelInstance to BindableArtboard (#11893) 1008488b6d
* feat(flutter): bind custom viewModelInstance to BindableArtboard

* chore: formatting

* test: add golden tests

* chore: clang

* test: null check first

* fix: store runtime VMI object on BindableArtboard to avoid early GC

* docs: add comment about lifetime for VMI on setting bindable artboards

fix(runtime): store relinked view model instance on nested artboard (#11931) 76c7232fa1

feature: add support for sending keyboard inputs to focused elements (#11924) 19486d13d0

Add support for setting ViewModel value using Listeners and comparing ViewModels in Transition Conditions (#11917) 46a089fc12
ViewModels can now be used in the following ways:

Setting ViewModel properties of type ViewModel to either: 1) another ViewModel property of type ViewModel or 2) the ViewModelInstance of the state machine's current data context
Comparing 2 ViewModel values in a transition condition using either 1) 2 different ViewModel properties of type ViewModel or 2) a ViewModel property of type ViewModel with the ViewModelInstance of the state machine's current data context.

chore: skip focus nodes that are collapsed or fully transparent (#11916) 57139fa664
* chore: skip focus nodes that are collapsed or fully transparent

Nnnnn library export fixes (#11884) 68176685ac
* ensure default instance is always exported and add all properties if no instance is

* reset isAdvanceActive when a script is initialized

feat(vkcwa): Implement advanced blend (#11858) 37c39e85d4
To implement advanced blend, we use an input attachment for the color
buffer and require barriers before paths with advanced blend, but are
able work out overlapping fragments within a single path with atomics
instead.

feature: enable data binding view model properties (#11867) 9325025e52
Adds support for data binding view model properties.
This feature is only available for view model instances so far, to support binding view models properties to stateful components.
It should be extended in a separate PR to state machine listeners and conditions.

Pass Layout Animation interpolator values into NestedArtboards and ArtboardComponentLists (#11877) c3cd2254cc
Adds support for properly passing layout animation interpolators down into nested artboard layouts and artboard component lists. For the Dart to C++ bridge, we only pass the interpolator values (rather than having to do extra work to generate C++ interpolators that need to get referenced from Dart) and let the interpolator be instanced in the binding. Then using those values, the C++ side can hold onto its own interpolator and cascade it down.

Bind ViewModelInstanceList with Stateful Components (#11878) e4abf64f44

fix: correct emoji image buffer size and dual-presentation fallback (#11876) 7640c5a47a

feature: add support for removing all elements from a view model list (#11872) 354acb8533
feature: add support for removing al elements from a view model list

feature: add support for multiple inputs on listeners (#11862) 501b7f488c
* feature: add support for multiple inputs on listeners

feature: emojis! (#11857) 89305a5fed
* feature: emojis!

* fix: pr feedback

* fix: missed font asset

* fix: more missed fonts

* feature: harfbuzz 13.1.1

* fix: removed too much

* fix: cleanup

* fix: goldens changed by text

* harfbuzz: even more removed

* fix: re-add harfbuzz feature

* chore: rebaseline goldens

* chore: strip HB_NO_VAR_COMPOSITES

* more font tests

* chore: rebaseline d3d atomic

* chore: pixel6 goldens rebaseline

* fix: test path

fix(runtime): Don't early out when processing KeyedObjects with missing objects (#11856) 3eb4211852
Previously when a LinearAnimation ran onAddedDirty on all of its keyed objects, it would return early if any keyed objects returned a status other than OK (this could happen in cases where the keyed object itself doesn't validate, for example, when a constraint doesn't have a target, and that constraint has a keyed property on the timeline). This PR updates the flow to instead store those keyed objects and after all keyed objects are processed, it removes them from the list, so they will not be processed in future calls to apply().

Component Input/Output Properties (#11854) 3034940065
Follow up to the Stateful Component Properties PR, this adds 2 things:

Adds a bitfield onto ViewModelProperty that allows properties to be set as input/output/both/none. I'm not sure we're going to use "both" but i put it in there in case. Currently its disabled, and the VM property has to be either an input or output. When a stateful Component's artboard is selected, we display an inspector where you can add ViewModel properties as inputs or outputs.
Makes ViewModel artboard and image properties bindable and animatable. ViewModel lists have a different mechanism, and will require additional work not in this PR.

fix(apple): support language hints, create font from tables (#11807) 30fcb84a44

feat(renderer): Enable reordering on clockwise mode (#11843) b5b724b93a
Batching improvements:

  marty.riv (x99): 1755 -> 978 batches
  Rope.riv: 217 -> 214 batches

(Not to mention the fact that going wide and combining non-overlapping
elements will result in, ideally, less contention for the per-pixel
raster order interlock.)

Stateful Component Properties (#11800) b09cb6b29e
The second step in implementing Stateful Components. When an artboard is marked isComponent=true and isStateful=true, and is placed in another artboard, we will generate a "stateful" ViewModelInstance based on the ViewModel bound to the Component artboard and add it as a child of the NestedArtboard (this is the ViewModelInstance that will be passed down into the source artboard instance). This ViewModelInstance can contain properties that will be exposed on the Component itself through a component properties inspector. Those properties (ViewModelInstance properties) can be modified directly, via keyframes or through databinding to other ViewModel properties. This stateful ViewModelInstance can also exist outside of the Artboard's ViewModel tree.

fix: dispose state machine when destroyed (#11820) ac3a79c78b

feature: add support for replacing view model property values (#11826) c8242bfa23
* feature: add support for replacing view model property values

commit.message:
fix: resolve all flake8 lint errors across vultron/ and test/

P1 (runtime crash) fixes:
- extractor.py: move VultronEvent/EVENT_CLASS_MAP/VultronTypes imports
  to module level; remove forward-reference string annotation
- helpers.py: remove spurious f-prefix from non-interpolating f-string
- robot.py: add missing _parse_args() function
- mcp_server.py: fix 9 use-case call signatures (SvcXxx(dl, req).execute())

P3/P4 (unused imports) fixes:
- Remove unused imports across 14 test files, 12 demo scripts, 5 vocab
  example files, and various adapter/use-case modules
- Restore BASE_URL in demo modules with # noqa: F401 (needed for test
  monkeypatching via mp.setattr)
- Fix F811 (logging redefinition) in test_embargo_use_cases.py
- Fix F841 (assigned-but-unused vars) in test_cli.py, test_create_tree.py,
  test_create_activity.py, and triggers/embargo.py

E402 (imports not at top) fixes:
- test_trigger_services.py: move FUTURE_DATETIME constant after all imports
- test_performance.py: move logger = ... after all imports; remove unused
  DataLayer import

E203 (.flake8):
- Add E203 to extend-ignore (Black-style slice whitespace)

All # noqa placement fixed to appear on the opening 'from X import (' line,
not on the closing ')' line (where flake8 ignores them).

Result: flake8 clean, 985 tests pass, Black unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs(paper): §8.13 knowledge barrier generalization

Add paragraph to §8.13 making explicit that syntactic expertise
depreciates universally — not just for popular frameworks:
- Poorly-documented tools: AI generates docs from source, add to context,
  RAG makes available in every session. Barrier = context-preparation, not
  months of familiarization.
- Value shift stated as clean thesis: tool-syntax/pattern knowledge
  depreciates across the board; deep domain + cross-domain synthesis
  appreciates. Premium moves from 'can implement' to 'can correctly specify.'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: generative-specification
repository.description: Generative Specification: experiments, evidence, white paper, and community quality gates flywheel

commit.message:
docs(paper): directionally convergent + §8.13 The Engineer Elevated

- §7.8: 'eventually convergent' → 'directionally convergent' (more precise:
  direction is unambiguous, path is not smooth)

- §8.13 new section: 'The Engineer Elevated'
  * GS is a convergence mechanism — but convergence assumes a correct spec
  * Complex/large projects require experts to define dimensions, create,
    curate, and expand the specification; oversight and domain judgment
    cannot be automated
  * Boilerplate/standard projects will be automated — same transition as
    frameworks automating boilerplate code (craft changes, not eliminated)
  * Engineer elevated to the harder problem: stating what must be true
    before code exists; implementation abundant, correct specification scarce

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: return early in outbox_handler when actor is None (BUG-001)

The actor-is-None guard logged a warning but fell through to
'while actor.outbox.items:', raising AttributeError. Add 'return'
immediately after the warning to short-circuit the rest of the
function.

Adds regression test: test_outbox_handler_returns_early_when_actor_not_found

commit.message:
Dual theme-color meta for light/dark status bar

Split the single theme-color meta into two with media queries so
the status bar adapts to light/dark on Safari tabs and iOS 18+ PWA.

Change apple-mobile-web-app-status-bar-style from black-translucent
(deprecated) to default, which follows system theme naturally and
lets theme-color control the actual color.

17 standalone PWA locks prefers-color-scheme at  nolaunch iOS
reliable workaround exists (confirmed by Apple Developer Forums).
Users on those versions must restart the app after changing system
theme. iOS 18+ handles this automatically.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[loganalyzer] Ignore link-training status query errors during port transitions (#23200)

SAI cannot read link-training failure/rx status registers while the port
serdes is mid-transition (admin toggle). This causes transient ERR logs
from getPortLinkTrainingFailure and getPortLinkTrainingRxStatus in
orchagent that are harmless and do not indicate a real problem.

Once the port stabilizes (up or down), the queries succeed via the
periodic poller. Add both error patterns to the common ignore list.

Signed-off-by: Dashuai Zhang <dashuaizhang@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix test_masked_services.py test when golden config is presented (#23183)

What is the motivation for this PR?
Some interfaces depend on physical-layer attributes in golden_config_db.json (e.g., link-training) to come up. Previously config_reload was called without override_config=True, so these settings were lost during minigraph load. This worked before but breaks on newer platforms that require these settings.

How did you do it?
Check if /etc/sonic/golden_config_db.json exists on the DUT. If yes, call config_reload with override_config=True; otherwise call without override to avoid failure.

How did you verify/test it?
Verified on physical testbed. Interfaces requiring link-training come up correctly when golden config is present.

Any platform specific information?
N/A

Signed-off-by: Dashuai Zhang <dashuaizhang@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs(paper): §7.3 factual corrections + §7.8 strengthened argument

§7.3 ForgeCraft case study:
- Version: v0.5.1 → 1.0.0 (released March 2026)
- Tests: 307 → 1127 (current)
- Distribution: npm + MCP client config (removed Homebrew/Snap/Chocolatey)
- Added open-source monetisation model (consulting, no subscription fee)

§7.8 Meta-Application:
- Added opening paragraph explicitly naming which cases used v0.5.1 vs what AX built
- Cycle expanded to 8 steps; step 1 now makes self-referential nature explicit
  (ForgeCraft is simultaneously specifier and subject of specification)
- Self-Applicability closing: added Completeness Law paragraph
  (S_realised = 1.0 at AX ceiling; law is self-consistent, not just directional)

Change 1 (version → 1.1) already applied in prior session.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Update .github/workflows/ci.yml

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(dx): scoring script bug fixes + re-run against all 6 pilot conditions

Fixes applied:
1. grep -> Node.js fs scan (cross-platform, no shell grep dependency)
2. --passWithNoTests removed; test count checked explicitly (0 tests = fail)
3. Coverage parsed from jest --coverage output; threshold 60% on feature file
4. rm -rf -> fs.rmSync(recursive) (cross-platform cleanup)
5. git clone --depth 1 -> --depth 20 (commit history check sees real history)
6. Bounded: checks new feature route only (digest.ts / activity.ts), not all routes

Re-run results (corrected gradient):
  naive:      1/7 automatable (scaffold commit artifact; real forks expected 0)
  competent:  3/7
  gs:         7/7 (perfect on all automatable checks)

GS achieves ceiling on automatable checks. Composable +3 (live server) is the
only remaining gap before April session.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(ui): include seed and config in exported game log

Log header now shows seed, player count, territory
count, map shape, speed, and enabled options. Player
list includes personality types for AI players.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(dx): add self-administered pilot results — baseline scores for April experiment

Six conditions piloted across both workshop repos:
- base-naive / kanban-naive: 0/14, Prisma in all routes, no tests
- base-competent / kanban-competent: 2-3/14, tests passing, no architecture
- base-gs / kanban-gs: 6-8/14, service layer, CLAUDE.md, ADRs, passing tests

Scoring script issues found (4 bugs, 2 calibration issues):
- Bounded check is a false positive on Windows (grep unavailable)
- Verifiable false positive when 0 tests + --passWithNoTests flag
- Coverage check not enforced in automation
- git --depth 1 makes commit format check unreliable

Rubric calibration: Bounded check fails for GS because pre-existing scaffold
routes carry Prisma. New feature routes (digest.ts, activity.ts) are clean.
Recommendation: check Bounded on new/changed files only.

All implementations in experiments/dx/pilot/. Results in results.md.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs(paper): add §7.8 Meta-Application — autonomous specification evolution

New section covering:
- AX experiment as simultaneous ForgeCraft development mechanism
- close_cycle audit loop: AI identifies gap, AI encodes fix, human validates
- Human role as trigger/validator, not author (git history verifiable)
- Eventually convergent (not monotone) — v4 regression explained as gap interaction
- F3 dependency rebuttal: ForgeCraft 1.0 emerged from AX, not prior to it
- Self-applicability as strongest implication: pragmatic tier governs its own instrument

Cross-references: §4.2 (pragmatic tier), §7.7 (F3 threat), §9.1 (agentic self-refinement), §10 (community ratchet)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
